title: LoL Esports |
content:
The battle for the 2026 LCS Championship is almost here, and fans can soon secure their spot to witness North America's best teams compete live.

On October 3-4, the 2026 LCS Championship Driven by Kia heads to Gas South Arena just outside Atlanta, Georgia, where the region's best teams will battle for the LCS Championship title and the opportunity to represent North America at the 2026 League of Legends World Championship.

Tickets for the 2026 LCS Championship Driven by Kia will be released in two waves through Ticketmaster.

The first wave of tickets will feature two-day passes only and will go on sale on Friday, July 24, at 10:00 AM PT / 1:00 PM ET.

Purchasing a two-day pass guarantees your seat for both days of competition, including Championship Sunday when one team will lift the LCS trophy and the top three teams will punch their ticket to Worlds. Wave 1 includes seats on the lower levels as well as optimal viewing sections.

The second wave of ticket sales will begin Saturday, August 22, at 10:00 AM PT / 1:00 PM ET. During this wave, fans will be able to purchase additional seating options for two-day passes (while supplies last) and single-day tickets for Saturday or Sunday.

title: Esports Foundation Chief Games Officer about the competition’s future: “Esports World Cup will travel”
summary: Esports Foundation Chief Games Officer about the competition’s future: “Esports World Cup will travel”  Hotspawn
content:
During the League of Legends tournament at the Esports World Cup, held in Paris from July 15 to 19, members of the media were invited to a roundtable discussion with Riot Games’ Head of League of Legends Esports, Chris Greeley, alongside Esports World Cup Foundation Chief Games Officer Fabian Scheuermann.

The session covered a wide range of topics, including informations about future Mid-Season Invitational and Esports World Cup events, the Foundation’s long-term vision, and their thoughts on the possibility of the two competitions eventually merging. Here’s what we learned.

While the EWC has become an integral part of the competitive calendar in several titles, such as EA SPORTS FC, whose World Championship will take place from July 22 to 26 at Porte de Versailles, for League of Legends, it remains an additional international event inserted between the MSI and the start of the regional Summer Splits.

Head of LoL Esports Chris Greeley 🇺🇸 on whether MSI and EWC could be merged moving forward

“We have MSI venues booked for 2027, and we know where we are going in 2028. It is not Riyadh. At least for the foreseeable future, we are planning to keep the events separate.

— Hotspawn LoL (@hotspawnlol)July 19, 2026

title: 5 Questions with AUR Huanmie, the Space Gods Champion!
summary: 5 Questions with AUR Huanmie, the Space Gods Champion!  Teamfight Tactics
content:
The stars have spoken, and a new Champion has risen! After three days of incredible competition at the Space Gods Tactician's Crown, AUR Huanmie representing CN emerged victorious, claiming the Tactician's Crown, the $150K grand prize, and the title of Space Gods Champion. Huanmie previously won the Set 5 Reckoning Championship as well as the most recent TFT Paris Open, making this his third international title.

After the celebratory confetti settled, we sat down with the Champion to get his perspective on the tournament:

Going into the final game, I saw that the Space God wasAhri, which made me realize I could play for a Level 9 composition. I also hit theCaretaker's Allyaugment, which is great for fast-leveling to 9. Even though it didn't give me the ideal units, I already had three Primordian units to generate extra gold, so I could still make it work. Then at 3-2, I foundForward Thinking, which let me reach Level 9 even faster. At the same time, the Riven units I found stabilized my Stage 3 board, and I was even able to activate 3 Timebreakers, which helped me build a win streak. Although my rolldown at Level 9 during Stage 4 wasn't very successful, I had built such a large advantage in the early game that I felt I had a real chance to win the championship.

I think I adapted to this set extremely well. After Day 1, even though my score wasn't very high, I felt that I had been playing well overall. Then after Day 2, I finished with one of the highest scores. That was when I realized I had a deep understanding of this set and that I had a great chance to become the champion.

I think it was the second-to-last game. After my opponentBensacwas eliminated, I realized that many of the units from the Space Groove composition would be returned to the pool, and I had saved up a lot of gold to roll. At that moment, I knew that if I could hit a three-star 4-cost unit, I would definitely make it to the final deciding game. I think recognizing that opportunity and committing to it was the best decision I made during the tournament.

If you truly love TFT, my advice is to find a group of close friends who are just as passionate about the game. Discuss strategies together, learn from each other, and improve together. Step by step, you'll give yourself a chance to compete on bigger and bigger stages.

title: DPlus KIA crowned 2026 League of Legends Esports World Cup champions after defeating Karmine Corp
summary: DPlus KIA crowned 2026 League of Legends Esports World Cup champions after defeating Karmine Corp  GosuGamers
content:
DPlus KIA are the EWC Champions (Image: Esports World Cup).

Dplus KIAhave been crowned theLeague of Legends (LoL)Esports World Cup (EWC) 2026champions after they defeatedKarmine Corpin a dominant 3-0 sweep at the tournament's Grand Final on Sunday (19 July).

Both teams reached the Grand Final after remarkable Cinderella Runs through the Playoffs. Karmine Corp upset LPL powerhouseAG.ALin the quarterfinals before sendingT1crashing out in front of a roaring home crowd. DPlus KIA, meanwhile, pulled off the tournament's biggest shock by knocking outBilibili Gamingbefore endingGen.G's bid for a third consecutive Esports World Cup title in the semifinals.

DPlus KIA found the early advantage, but Karmine Corp refused to let the game slip away without a fight, repeatedly contesting objectives and preventing DPlus from building an insurmountable gold lead. Even so,Heo “ShowMaker” Su's Viktor andSmash's Kai'Sa proved pivotal in keeping DPlus in control as they gradually tightened its grip across the map. After 31 minutes, DPlus marched into KC's base and destroyed the Nexus with a 16-9 kill lead to take the opening game of the series.

The second game began with both teams trading blows and keeping the gold nearly even through the early and mid game. The turning point came around the Baron pit, where DPlus KIA emerged victorious from a decisive team fight to wrest control of the map. With their lead growing, Smash took over the Rift, spearheading DPlus' relentless push as the LCK side won fight after fight. After 33 minutes and an 18-8 kill lead, DPlus stormed into KC's base and destroyed the Nexus to claim Game 2, putting themselves on match point.

DPlus KIA came into the third game determined to close out the series. The LCK side played aggressively from the opening minutes, picking up early kills and bullying KC across the map during the laning phase. Their relentless pressure set the French side's bottom lane behind while preventing their jungler from finding the space to scale.

title: League of Legends Esports World Cup 2026: Schedule, format, teams and how to watch
summary: League of Legends Esports World Cup 2026: Schedule, format, teams and how to watch  DAZN
content:
The competitive League of Legends ecosystem is buzzing following the latest announcement regarding 'League of Legends Esports World Cup 2026: Schedule, format, teams and how to watch'. As the season progresses, this development has quickly taken center stage, drawing intense scrutiny from fans, analysts, and professional players alike. The core update highlights a significant shift that is expected to have wide-ranging consequences for the regional leagues and international tournaments.

Riot Games continues to refine the competitive structure, balancing the game's high skill ceiling with spectator entertainment. The professional scene is an ever-evolving battleground where even the smallest adjustment can make or break a team's championship aspirations. Every coach, analyst, and player must stay ahead of the curve to remain competitive.

Speaking on the recent events, seasoned esports analysts noted that 'League of Legends Esports World Cup 2026: Schedule, format, teams and how to watch  DAZN' is not just a temporary trend but a fundamental shift in how teams will approach their upcoming matchdays. 'Draft priority and map control are going to be completely redefined over the next few weeks,' remarked a prominent league caster. 'Teams that fail to recognize the gravity of this change will find themselves falling behind very quickly in the standings.'

As the community awaits the upcoming weekend matches, anticipation continues to build. Fans can catch all the high-stakes action live on official streaming channels, complete with expert desk analysis and live regional broadcasts. With so much on the line, the next chapter of the 2026 competitive season promises to deliver some of the most memorable moments in League of Legends esports history.
